segments.text: Then Zophar the Naamathite replied:
Should this stream of words go unanswered and such a speaker be vindicated?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Should your babbling put others to silence? Will you scoff without rebuke?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: You have said, ‘My doctrine is sound, and I am pure in Your sight.’
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: But if only God would speak and open His lips against you,
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: and disclose to you the secrets of wisdom, for true wisdom has two sides. Know then that God exacts from youless than your iniquity deserves.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Can you fathom the deep things of Godor discover the limits of the Almighty?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: They are higher than the heavens—what can you do? They are deeper than Sheol—what can you know?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Their measure is longer than the earth and wider than the sea.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: If He comes along to imprison you, or convenes a court, who can stop Him?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Surely He knows the deceit of men. If He sees iniquity, does He not take note?
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: But a witless man can no more become wisethan the colt of a wild donkey can be born a man!
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: As for you, if you direct your heart and lift up your hands to Him,
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: if you put away the iniquity in your hand, and allow no injustice to dwell in your tents,
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: then indeed you will lift up your face without shame; you will st and firm and unafraid.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: For you will forget your misery, recalling it only as waters gone by.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: Your life will be brighter than noonday; its darkness will be like the morning.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: You will be secure, because there is hope, and you will look around and lie down in safety.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text: You will lie down without fear, and many will court your favor.
segments.speaker: Zophar

segments.text:
But the eyes of the wicked will fail, and escape will elude them;
they will hope for their last breath.
segments.speaker: Zophar
